steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The first sentence states a broad principle: energy transfers across trophic levels. The second sentence then gives concrete examples of that principle in action (plants to herbivores, herbivores to carnivores, etc.). The blank must signal that what follows illustrates the preceding claim.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — However,: signals contrast or contradiction. The second sentence doesn't contradict the first — it demonstrates it. ✗
  • Choice B — Likewise,: signals a parallel statement of equal weight. The second sentence is an example of the first, not a parallel claim. ✗
  • Choice C — For example,: signals that the following content illustrates the preceding claim. That is exactly the relationship here. ✓
  • Choice D — By contrast,: signals opposition. No opposition exists between the two sentences. ✗
C is correct.
